name: RedPajama-INCITE-7B-Chat
note: Chat variant fine-tuned on OASST1 + Dolly v2 via OpenChatKit, kept free of distilled proprietary data.

results.notes: Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard (v1) lm-eval-harness run for togethercomputer/RedPajama-INCITE-7B-Chat: harness|arc:challenge|25 acc_norm = 0.42065 (42.06%). Exact-model result, not a sibling checkpoint.
Same HF Open LLM Leaderboard (v1) run: harness|hellaswag|10 acc_norm = 0.70823 (70.82%).

Same run: 5-shot acc averaged across all 57 hendrycksTest (MMLU) subject-level results in the raw JSON (recomputed by hand from the per-subject acc values since the file has no single aggregate MMLU field); result = 26.94%, essentially chance level, consistent with a small non-MMLU-tuned 2023 chat model.
Follow-up HF Open LLM Leaderboard supplemental run (adding winogrande/gsm8k/drop to earlier-evaluated models): winogrande acc = 0.59826 (59.83%).

Same supplemental run: gsm8k acc = 0.00455 (0.45%). Near-zero — a known lm-eval-harness answer-extraction limitation for small 2023 non-reasoning chat models on this leaderboard version, but it is the genuine recorded score for this exact checkpoint, included for completeness. [score corrected during adversarial verify: proposed 0.45 -> 0.0045, per verifier: Fetched the raw source JSON directly (results_2023-10-19T02-47-12.906889.json for togethercomputer/RedPajama-INCITE-7B-Chat). Confirmed model name matches exactly and the harness|gsm8k|5 entry is present and correctly attributed (no benchmark-variant or model mixup). However, the stored value is acc=0.004548900682335102 (i.e., 0.45% / 0.00455 as a fraction), not 0.45. The claimed score of 0.45 appears to be a units error — someone likely took "0.45%" and dropped the percent sign rather than converting it to a 0-1 fraction (0.45% = 0.0045, not 0.45). This is a genuine, low but real recorded score for this exact checkpoint (a known lm-eval-harness answer-extraction issue for small 2023 non-reasoning chat models), so it should be recorded as 0.0045 (or 0.00455 for full precision), not 0.45, to stay consistent with standard 0-1 fraction units used elsewhere in the corpus.]

verificationNotes: Confirmed real model released alongside 7B-Base/Instruct per together.ai/blog/redpajama-7b (June 6, 2023), same 6.9B param count, GPT-NeoX architecture, and Apache 2.0 license as sibling 7B checkpoints. No results claimed, none added (no HELM score for Chat variant found in the blog or arxiv paper).
